---
title: "Foundations: Core Geospatial Concepts"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Planned guide -- not yet written.** This is the spine of the series; the other
guides assume the vocabulary introduced here.
:::

## Intended scope

A from-scratch tour of the geospatial ideas an EO software engineer keeps
bumping into, each explained at a practical level with a concept diagram:

- **The Earth is not flat (or a sphere).** Geoid vs. ellipsoid, datums, and why
  "latitude/longitude" needs a datum to mean anything.
- **Coordinate Reference Systems (CRS).** Geographic vs. projected, the OGC CRS
  URI scheme, EPSG codes, and the longitude/latitude order trap.
- **Projections.** Why flattening a curved surface always distorts something,
  and the trade-offs (Web Mercator vs. equal-area, etc.).
- **Raster vs. vector.** Grids of cells vs. discrete geometry features.
- **Coverages.** The "field of values over a domain" model that underlies
  imagery, elevation, and climate data (see the
  [CoverageJSON guide](../formats/coveragejson.qmd)).
- **Footprints and point data.** Swaths, scenes, and instruments like GEDI that
  sample the surface as points/footprints rather than full rasters.
- **Tiles and resolution.** Tiling schemes, zoom levels, and overviews.
